title: Introduction to Statistics

jokes.text: Little-known fact, statistics were not his only love. He's also famous for his french sauce used on meat and poultry.
jokes.setup: Jeff tries to impress with a 'little-known fact' about Bernoulli
jokes.punchline: Confuses Bernoulli with béarnaise sauce
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff move - trying to sound smart and failing. Clean setup/payoff with good wordplay between mathematical concept and sauce.

jokes.text: you were nailing it, until you had to ask.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks if he's being sarcastic or nailing his confident approach
jokes.punchline: She reveals asking that question undermined his confidence act
jokes.explanation: Perfect paradox of confidence - asking if you're confident proves you're not. Economical and insightful about dating psychology.

jokes.text: oh, damn it. starting over. Hey, you in the skirt, date me.
jokes.setup: Jeff realizes he messed up his confident approach
jokes.punchline: Resets to comically blunt, caveman-like directness
jokes.explanation: Jeff's ego can't handle failure so he overcorrects to absurd bluntness. Good character beat showing his immaturity.

jokes.text: i've graded enough of your tests to know i'd never feel mentally inadequate.
jokes.setup: Slater lists Jeff's attractive qualities
jokes.punchline: Reveals she knows he's not very smart from his test scores
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - starts as compliment, ends as devastating burn. Shows Jeff's academic struggles while establishing Slater's wit.

jokes.text: Did you ever see that katherine heigl movie poster where gerard butler had that heart over his weiner? That resonated with me.
jokes.setup: Britta complaining about men's behavior
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific movie poster reference with crude description
jokes.explanation: Very specific pop culture reference with unexpected crudeness. Britta's pseudo-feminist analysis of rom-com imagery is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I'm barely a student. I'm older than you. I drive a lexus. i saw ghost busters in the theater. Look, my gums are receding.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to prove he's not really a student
jokes.punchline: Escalates from status symbols to aging body parts
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from materialistic bragging to desperate physical evidence. Shows Jeff's vanity and desperation simultaneously.

jokes.text: it's uncanny how many of my buttons you're finding, But i have a personal rule about this, and i stick to it.
jokes.setup: Jeff's increasingly desperate attempts to seem mature
jokes.punchline: She admits he's working but maintains boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good tension between attraction and professional boundaries. 'Finding buttons' is a nice euphemism for his effect on her.

jokes.text: then i can mark you down as definitely being there From 7:00 sharp till upside-down spanish question mark?
jokes.setup: Annie trying to pin Jeff down about party attendance
jokes.punchline: Describes party end time as 'upside-down spanish question mark'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant linguistic humor - not knowing how to say '¿' so describing it visually. Very Annie-like overthinking of simple concepts.

jokes.text: infomercials work on pierce.
jokes.setup: Annie claims her assertiveness worked on Pierce
jokes.punchline: Jeff points out Pierce is easily manipulated by TV ads
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that completely deflate Annie's confidence about her persuasive powers by referencing Pierce's gullibility.

jokes.text: The last time you did this, I saved a vial of your tears And have been slowly building up an immunity.
jokes.setup: Annie trying emotional manipulation with sad story
jokes.punchline: Jeff claims he's literally collecting and using her tears to build immunity
jokes.explanation: Fantastic absurdist metaphor treating emotional manipulation like poison immunity. Shows Jeff's cynicism through ridiculous scientific analogy.

jokes.text: i was so unpopular in high school, The crossing guard used to lure me into traffic.
jokes.setup: Annie explaining her unpopularity
jokes.punchline: Implies crossing guard actively tried to get her hit by cars
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about institutional authority figures wanting her dead. Absurd escalation of unpopularity that's both funny and pathetic.

jokes.text: My grandpa took that when he was around your age... one time he started hallucinating And ran down the street with no pants on. Which in the gaza strip is considered a real party foul
jokes.setup: Abed warning Pierce about his medication with grandfather story
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Gaza Strip as location for pants-less incident
jokes.explanation: Typical Abed - drops serious geopolitical reference casually into silly story. 'Party foul' understatement for cultural violation is nice touch.

jokes.text: look, i really don't need your advice, And i'm not your pantsless grandpa.
jokes.setup: Pierce dismissing Abed's medication warning
jokes.punchline: References the specific 'pantsless grandpa' detail
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Abed's story. Pierce's indignation at being compared to pantsless elderly man is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: you don't have to keep doing that!
jokes.setup: Annie keeps adding Spanish translations after English phrases
jokes.punchline: Britta's exasperated reaction to the unnecessary translations
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Britta's practical frustration with Annie's over-enthusiasm for cultural accuracy.

jokes.text: she blew you off, huh?
jokes.setup: Jeff gives flowery speech about friendship
jokes.punchline: Annie immediately sees through to the real reason he's there
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Jeff's pretentious excuse. Annie's directness cuts through his BS immediately with economy and insight.

jokes.text: oh, look, it's the eavesdropping matador.
jokes.setup: Chang appears and reveals information about Slater
jokes.punchline: Jeff insults both Chang's costume and his eavesdropping
jokes.explanation: Nice combination of costume reference with behavioral criticism. Jeff's defensiveness expressed through creative insult.

jokes.text: are you saying my people are sneaky?
jokes.setup: Jeff calls Chang an 'eavesdropping matador'
jokes.punchline: Chang interprets this as racial stereotype about Spanish people
jokes.explanation: Chang's paranoia about racial implications where none exist. Misdirection from the actual behavior Jeff was criticizing.

jokes.text: am i good looking? - you're a very attractive young man. - i knew it.
jokes.setup: Troy asks Batman/Abed if he's good looking
jokes.punchline: Troy's satisfied confidence after getting compliment from friend in costume
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing Troy's insecurity and need for validation, even from costumed Abed. His confidence boost is endearing.

jokes.text: i am the beastmaster. from the movie beastmaster. What rock have you been living under?
jokes.setup: Pierce introduces himself as Beastmaster
jokes.punchline: His shock that someone doesn't know obscure 80s fantasy movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce behavior - assuming everyone shares his pop culture references and being indignant when they don't.

jokes.text: What are you going as? A gay douchebag? just kidding.
jokes.setup: Pierce asking about Jeff's non-costume
jokes.punchline: Offensive joke followed by weak 'just kidding' disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad joke that shows Pierce's outdated humor and social insensitivity. The cringe is the point, not clever writing.

jokes.text: oh, thanks, urkel. - i'm harry potter. - really? - what up, urkel?
jokes.setup: Abed dressed as Harry Potter brings drinks
jokes.punchline: Everyone calls him Urkel despite correction, Troy joins in
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about costume misidentification. Shows how pop culture references can be generational and imprecise.

jokes.text: I've got 'ludes, uh, dreamers... Johnny boys.
jokes.setup: Pierce offering to share drugs with Star-Burns
jokes.punchline: Made-up drug names that sound like old slang
jokes.explanation: Pierce making up drug names to sound cool. 'Johnny boys' is particularly absurd. Shows his disconnect from actual drug culture.

jokes.text: oh, sorry. I thought i was dealing with the beastmaster.
jokes.setup: Pierce hesitating about drug trade
jokes.punchline: Star-Burns challenges Pierce's commitment to his costume persona
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Star-Burns uses Pierce's costume identity to manipulate him into drug deal. Smart character psychology.

jokes.text: Professor slater doesn't date students. Or married asians who drive mopeds.
jokes.setup: Chang giving Jeff dating advice about Slater
jokes.punchline: Reveals his own failed attempt with oddly specific personal details
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Chang's bitterness revealed through specific details of his own rejection. Moped detail is perfect Chang touch.

jokes.text: hey, uh, man? what'd you slip me? I keep grinding my teeth, and i want to kiss everybody.
jokes.setup: Pierce took unknown drugs from Star-Burns
jokes.punchline: Describes classic MDMA/ecstasy symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Pierce's drug subplot. Specific symptoms are accurate and funny coming from elderly character.

jokes.text: My heart stopped racing, and i can't pee.
jokes.setup: Pierce continuing to describe drug effects
jokes.punchline: More specific and embarrassing physical symptoms
jokes.explanation: Continues drug effects subplot. TMI moment about urination is appropriately uncomfortable for Pierce character.

jokes.text: to be with professor short skirt? We hate her.
jokes.setup: Learning Jeff went to faculty party
jokes.punchline: Shirley immediately declares group hatred for Slater
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley reaction - immediate tribal loyalty and slut-shaming. 'Short skirt' shows her judgmental side.

jokes.text: We should go find that bitch's car And snap off her antenna.
jokes.setup: Shirley expressing hatred for Slater
jokes.punchline: Suggests specific, petty act of vandalism
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Shirley's passive-aggression turning actively destructive. Antenna detail shows her pettiness.

jokes.text: That's right, professor slim calves. This is what you get when you steal jeff from a good woman!
jokes.setup: Abed vandalizing Professor Slater's car antenna
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific insult and dramatic declaration
jokes.explanation: Great Abed moment - 'slim calves' is such a weirdly specific insult. His dramatic delivery of soap opera dialogue is perfect.

jokes.text: I may or may not have been deflowered By a junior rodeo champion.
jokes.setup: Slater commenting on Jeff's cowboy outfit
jokes.punchline: Overshares very specific sexual history
jokes.explanation: Unexpected overshare with specific detail. Shows Slater's directness and creates awkward intimacy.

jokes.text: you lost your virginity to robin hood?
jokes.setup: Slater mentions losing virginity, Jeff is in costume
jokes.punchline: Jeff misunderstands, thinks she means his Robin Hood costume
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Jeff's ego makes him think she's referring to him. Shows his self-centeredness.

jokes.text: what friends? I have no friends. I hate everyone here but you.
jokes.setup: Slater worried about Jeff telling friends about relationship
jokes.punchline: Jeff's desperate claim of friendlessness as romantic pitch
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment - turning antisocial behavior into romantic gesture. The desperation makes it both funny and sad.

jokes.text: i think the words that you're looking for Are 'i own a cowboy outfit.' And it is tight too. did you buy it like that? Your toy gun to my head, i'd say yes.
jokes.setup: Chang questioning where Jeff got costume
jokes.punchline: Implies Jeff owns tight cowboy outfit and makes gun pun
jokes.explanation: Chang's harassment escalates with sexual implications and pun on 'gun to my head.' Shows his antagonistic relationship with Jeff.

jokes.text: britta perry, g.e.d.
jokes.setup: Slater introduces herself with PhD credentials
jokes.punchline: Britta counters with GED (high school equivalency)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - two words that perfectly establish class/education dynamic. Britta's defiant delivery makes it even better.

jokes.text: when you say 'classmate,' It sounds like we take naps together and eat paste
jokes.setup: Slater refers to Britta as Jeff's classmate
jokes.punchline: Britta objects to infantilizing implications of 'classmate'
jokes.explanation: Good linguistic humor - Britta's insecurity about being seen as childish student vs adult. Paste-eating is perfect kindergarten reference.

jokes.text: it's like grumpy old men but not hilarious.
jokes.setup: Troy describing Pierce's drug-induced behavior
jokes.punchline: References comedy movie but says real version isn't funny
jokes.explanation: Nice pop culture reference with realistic qualifier. Troy's observation that real elderly drug use isn't comedic shows maturity.

jokes.text: why is urkel ripping the antenna Off professor slater's car?
jokes.setup: Chang observing Abed vandalizing car
jokes.punchline: Still calling Abed 'Urkel' while describing Shirley's revenge plan in action
jokes.explanation: Good callback to both running gags. The casual way Chang observes vandalism while using wrong name is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: That's right, professor slim calves. This is what you get when you steal jeff from a good woman!
jokes.setup: Abed vandalizing Slater's car
jokes.punchline: Delivers Shirley's message with oddly specific body part insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - delivering someone else's revenge with his characteristic specificity. 'Slim calves' is wonderfully weird insult.

jokes.text: i'm old? What do you mean old? Whose hands are these?
jokes.setup: Pierce being told he's too old to be tripping
jokes.punchline: Drug-induced existential crisis about his own body
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Pierce's drug trip. The progression from denial to body dysmorphia is both funny and disturbing.

jokes.text: i know i'm not batman. You could try not being a jerk.
jokes.setup: Jeff dismissing Abed's Batman act
jokes.punchline: Abed's calm, direct response calling Jeff out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed response - acknowledges reality while calling out Jeff's cruelty. Simple but effective moral center moment.

jokes.text: i'm putting this hose in her desk, And you going outside to turn the water on.
jokes.setup: Shirley planning revenge on Slater
jokes.punchline: Elaborate water damage scheme for professor's office
jokes.explanation: Shows Shirley's vindictive side through elaborate revenge plan. The practicality of her vandalism is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: the long-neck, weave-having bank teller Didn't feel universal.
jokes.setup: Shirley ranting about 'long-neck weave-having bank teller'
jokes.punchline: Britta points out the oddly specific nature of the insult
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary on Shirley's projection. Britta's therapist-like observation about specificity reveals emotional displacement.

jokes.text: Nice and pretentious.
jokes.setup: Britta suggesting Slater is probably nice after seeing her awards
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself with sarcastic judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - brief attempt at charity immediately undermined by jealousy. Shows her internal contradiction.

jokes.text: hey, man, what's going on? You have a full-on erection. Just rela-- just relax.
jokes.setup: Troy finding Pierce in compromising drug-induced state
jokes.punchline: Awkward observation about Pierce's physical arousal
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable physical comedy moment. Troy's innocent directness makes awkward situation worse. Drug-induced arousal is cringe territory.

jokes.text: I've got the body of a fifth grader, okay? If i was working with what you've got, She'd be at the comfort inn right now Giving me a mexican halloween.
jokes.setup: Chang comparing his dating prospects to Jeff's
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of hypothetical sexual encounter with cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Chang's self-awareness about his physical shortcomings paired with crude fantasy. 'Mexican Halloween' is weird but fits his character.

jokes.text: Please sleep with me. please. Pretty please. i'm so lonely. I haven't slept with anyone in a very long time, And you are so good looking. Please do me the favor of having sex with me.
jokes.setup: Chang asking what Jeff's secret move is
jokes.punchline: Chang's pathetically honest and desperate plea to imaginary woman
jokes.explanation: Peak Chang pathetic-ness. The escalation from 'please' to 'pretty please' to full desperate monologue is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: i never saw beastmaster. I just wanted to be cool.
jokes.setup: Pierce's been claiming to be the Beastmaster all episode
jokes.punchline: Drug-induced confession that he doesn't know the reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Pierce's costume choice. His vulnerability about wanting to be cool is both sad and funny. Great character revelation.

jokes.text: i know in the last two months You've probably doubled the national average For amount of life lived per lifetime.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to comfort Pierce about being old
jokes.punchline: Statistical metaphor for Pierce's recent life experiences
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Jeff moment - using his lawyer-speak for genuine comfort. 'Amount of life lived per lifetime' is poetic and specific.

jokes.text: If life is just a series of ridiculous attempts To be alive, You're a hero To everything that's ever lived.
jokes.setup: Jeff continuing his philosophical comfort to Pierce
jokes.punchline: Reframes Pierce's ridiculous behavior as heroic life affirmation
jokes.explanation: One of Jeff's finest moments - profound philosophical reframe that's both funny and genuinely moving. Perfect character growth.

jokes.text: I must be out there in the night, Staying vigilant. Wherever a party needs to be saved, I'm there. Wherever there are masks, Wherever there's tomfoolery and joy, I'm there.
jokes.setup: Annie asking if Batman is staying for party
jokes.punchline: Abed's elaborate Batman monologue about party protection duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed Batman performance. Mixing heroic vigilante speak with party protection is wonderfully absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: But sometimes i'm not, 'cause i'm out in the night, Staying vigilant, watching, Lurking, running, jumping, Hurtling, sleeping... No, i can't sleep. You sleep. i'm awake. I don't sleep. i don't blink. Am i a bird? no. I'm a bat. I am batman. Or am i? Yes, i am batman.
jokes.setup: Abed continuing Batman monologue
jokes.punchline: Stream of consciousness spiral including existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation into psychological breakdown while maintaining Batman voice. The bird/bat confusion and identity crisis are perfect Abed touches.

jokes.text: You sound like cookie monster. - i'm batman. - that's batman.
jokes.setup: Abed doing Batman voice
jokes.punchline: Troy's comparison to Cookie Monster, Abed's insistence, Troy's acceptance
jokes.explanation: Sweet Troy/Abed moment. Troy's immediate acceptance after correction shows their friendship dynamic perfectly.

jokes.text: candy corn looks like tiny traffic cones. - yeah, it sure does. - at, like, a candy traffic school.
jokes.setup: Pierce making stoned observation about candy corn
jokes.punchline: Develops into elaborate traffic school metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good stoner humor - Pierce's visual observation develops into absurd scenario. Shows his drug-altered perspective.

jokes.text: like, a little gingerbread man at the wheel, And he's drunk. - look out. - you'd be driving, But you keep wanting to eat yourself.
jokes.setup: Building on candy traffic school concept
jokes.punchline: Drunk gingerbread driver who wants to eat himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of candy metaphor. The self-cannibalization driving hazard is wonderfully absurd collaborative humor.

jokes.text: what is? - if i ever, like, woke up as a donut. - you would eat yourself? - i wouldn't even question it. that'd be tasty.
jokes.setup: Pierce mentions one of his biggest fears
jokes.punchline: Would immediately eat himself if transformed into donut
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist escalation. Pierce's matter-of-fact approach to self-cannibalization is both disturbing and hilarious.

tmdb_overview: Jeff sets his sights on an attractive professor, and Annie looks to boost her social standing by hosting a Day of the Dead party.
insight: Pierce's paintball obsession escalates from harmless to genuinely unhinged across 55 jokes.
